description: A child holding one position or water without a ripple is 'moesha'. The word looks only at movement on the scale that matters. 'maeli' (quiet) concerns sound, while 'shena' (calm) concerns agitation.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and carry the resonance into rounded mid-back 'o'. Break cleanly before a separate front 'e', which takes the stress. Raise the tongue blade toward the postalveolar region for voiceless 'sh', then open into central 'a'.
usage_notes: The established phrases 'moesha meilo' and 'moesha ruemi' mean sit still and lie still. The relevant body or surface holds its position.

semantic_domains.physical: absence of movement at the relevant bodily or material scale
